Project: jsonapi
Language: go
Description: A small, idiomatic, STANDARD-LIBRARY-ONLY JSON HTTP endpoint. No external dependencies — use encoding/json and net/http only. All files use package main.
Go module path: guildlm.dev/jsonapi

TARGET_FILE: main.go
Purpose of this file: package main. POST /echo accepts a JSON body {"message": string} and responds with JSON {"echo": <message>, "length": <len of message>} and Content-Type application/json. Return 405 for non-POST, 400 for invalid JSON or an empty message, 200 on success. Expose newMux() *http.ServeMux so tests can drive it. Standard library only (encoding/json, net/http).

All files in this project:
  - go.mod: Go module file. Module path guildlm.dev/jsonapi, go 1.23. No dependencies. Output as a ```mod block, not Go.
  - main.go: package main. POST /echo accepts a JSON body {"message": string} and responds with JSON {"echo": <message>, "length": <len of message>} and Content-Type application/json. Return 405 for non-POST, 400 for invalid JSON or an empty message, 200 on success. Expose newMux() *http.ServeMux so tests can drive it. Standard library only (encoding/json, net/http).
  - main_test.go: package main. Tests using net/http/httptest and encoding/json: POST a valid message returns 200 with the echoed message and correct length; POST an empty message returns 400. Build the handler with newMux(). Standard library only.


Already-written files in THIS package (same directory):
--- go.mod ---
module guildlm.dev/jsonapi

go 1.23


The SAME-PACKAGE files above (same directory) are part of THIS package. Every function, type, constant and variable they declare already exists — call and reference them directly, and do NOT redeclare or reimplement any of them (a Go 'redeclared in this block' error). For test files: do not paste copies of the functions under test, just call them.
USE SIBLING TYPES EXACTLY AS DECLARED: match struct vs interface. If a shared type is an interface, hold and pass it BY VALUE (`s Store`), never as a pointer `*Store` — a pointer-to-interface has no methods and will not compile. Call the methods that actually exist; do not invent method names.

ROUTING (Go 1.22+ ServeMux): register each route as METHOD + space + pattern, e.g. mux.HandleFunc("GET /tasks", h.List) and mux.HandleFunc("POST /tasks", h.Create). NEVER register the same bare pattern twice (two mux.HandleFunc("/tasks", ...) calls) — ServeMux PANICS at startup. Read path wildcards like {id} via r.PathValue("id").

IMPLEMENT EVERY function and method FULLY with real working logic — do NOT stub or leave placeholders (no `return ErrNotImplemented`, no `panic("not implemented")`, no TODO). If several methods are similar (e.g. the Project methods mirror the Task methods), write out each one completely. Never reference a symbol you have not defined or imported.

Write the complete contents of main.go. Use only the Go standard library. Output one fenced ```go block.

Project: numkit
Language: go
Description: A tiny, idiomatic, stdlib-only Go package named "numkit" with two pure integer functions and table-driven tests. No external dependencies, no unicode. All files use package numkit. Ground-truth values are plain arithmetic the tests can state exactly.
Go module path: guildlm.dev/numkit

TARGET_FILE: numkit.go
Purpose of this file: package numkit. GCD(a, b int) int returns the greatest common divisor of the absolute values of a and b using the Euclidean algorithm; GCD(0,0) is 0. Clamp(x, lo, hi int) int returns x bounded to the inclusive range [lo, hi]; if lo > hi the function returns x unchanged.

All files in this project:
  - go.mod: Go module file. Module path guildlm.dev/numkit, go 1.23. No dependencies. Output as a ```mod block, not Go.
  - numkit.go: package numkit. GCD(a, b int) int returns the greatest common divisor of the absolute values of a and b using the Euclidean algorithm; GCD(0,0) is 0. Clamp(x, lo, hi int) int returns x bounded to the inclusive range [lo, hi]; if lo > hi the function returns x unchanged.
  - numkit_test.go: package numkit. Table-driven tests for GCD and Clamp covering: GCD of coprime numbers (GCD(8,15)=1), a common factor (GCD(12,18)=6), a zero operand (GCD(0,5)=5), and negatives (GCD(-12,18)=6); Clamp below range (Clamp(-1,0,10)=0), inside range (Clamp(5,0,10)=5), above range (Clamp(99,0,10)=10), and inverted bounds returning x unchanged (Clamp(7,10,0)=7).

Project: taskapi
Language: go
Description: A real, production-shaped, STANDARD-LIBRARY-ONLY Go backend laid out as a proper multi-package project (cmd/ + internal/), the way an experienced Go engineer structures a service. No external dependencies whatsoever: net/http with its Go 1.22 ServeMux method routing, log/slog for STRUCTURED logging, context for cancellation, encoding/json, sync. Clean layering with dependency injection: config -> models -> store (repository interface + memory impl) -> api (handlers + middleware + router) -> cmd/server (composition root). Each package has ONE clear responsibility; packages depend inward only. Cross-package symbols are used by importing the package and qualifying them (models.Task, store.Store, store.ErrNotFound). Only exported (capitalised) names cross package boundaries.
Go module path: guildlm.dev/taskapi

TARGET_FILE: internal/config/config.go
Purpose of this file: package config. A Config struct { Addr string; ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, ShutdownTimeout time.Duration } and func Load() Config that reads env vars (os.Getenv) with sensible defaults (Addr ":8080", timeouts 5s/10s/10s), parsing durations/addresses defensively. Self-contained: imports only os, time (and strconv if needed). No other project package.

All files in this project:
  - go.mod: Go module file. Module path guildlm.dev/taskapi, go 1.23. No dependencies. Output as a ```mod block, not Go.
  - internal/config/config.go: package config. A Config struct { Addr string; ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, ShutdownTimeout time.Duration } and func Load() Config that reads env vars (os.Getenv) with sensible defaults (Addr ":8080", timeouts 5s/10s/10s), parsing durations/addresses defensively. Self-contained: imports only os, time (and strconv if needed). No other project package.
  - internal/models/models.go: package models. Domain types and their validation. Task struct {ID, Title, Status, ProjectID string} and Project struct {ID, Name string}, all with JSON tags. Valid Task.Status is "todo"|"doing"|"done". A sentinel var ErrInvalid = errors.New("invalid") (exported, matchable with errors.Is). Methods (t Task) Validate() error and (p Project) Validate() error that wrap ErrInvalid via fmt.Errorf("%w: ...") when Title/Name is empty or Status is not allowed. Imports only errors, fmt. No other project package.
  - internal/store/store.go: package store. The repository abstraction. Import the models package (guildlm.dev/taskapi/internal/models) and use models.Task / models.Project (qualified — do NOT redeclare them here). Exported sentinels var ErrNotFound and var ErrExists (errors.New, matchable with errors.Is). A Store INTERFACE with CreateTask(models.Task) error, GetTask(id string) (models.Task, error), ListTasks() []models.Task, DeleteTask(id string) error, and the same four for Project. Imports errors and the models package only.
  - internal/store/memory.go: package store. A goroutine-safe in-memory implementation of the Store interface from store.go (same package — reference Store, ErrNotFound, ErrExists directly, do NOT redeclare them). MemStore struct holding two maps (tasks, projects) keyed by ID guarded by one sync.RWMutex, built by NewMemStore() *MemStore. Create returns ErrExists on duplicate id; Get/Delete return ErrNotFound; List returns items sorted by ID. Use models.Task/models.Project (import the models package). A compile-time var _ Store = (*MemStore)(nil) assertion. Imports sort, sync, and the models package.
  - internal/store/memory_test.go: package store. Table/flow tests for MemStore (same package — call NewMemStore, Store methods, ErrNotFound, ErrExists directly; use models.Task via the imported models package). Each case builds a FRESH NewMemStore (never shared). Cover create->nil then duplicate->ErrExists (errors.Is); Get present->value / missing->ErrNotFound; List is id-sorted; Delete present->nil then Get->ErrNotFound; Delete missing->ErrNotFound. Run race-clean. Imports errors, testing, and the models package.
  - internal/api/response.go: package api. Small HTTP/JSON helpers reused by every handler: writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v any) sets Content-Type application/json and encodes v; writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, msg string) writes a JSON {"error": msg}; decodeJSON(r *http.Request, dst any) error decodes the body and returns an error on malformed input. Imports encoding/json, net/http only. No other project package.
  - internal/api/middleware.go: package api. Middleware type func(http.Handler) http.Handler and Chain(h http.Handler, mws ...Middleware) http.Handler where the first listed is outermost. A Logging(logger *slog.Logger) Middleware that logs method, path, resolved status (via a statusRecorder wrapping http.ResponseWriter, default 200) and duration using STRUCTURED log/slog (logger.Info with slog attributes). A Recover(logger *slog.Logger) Middleware turning a downstream panic into a 500 and logging it. Imports log/slog, net/http, time. No other project package.
  - internal/api/tasks.go: package api. TaskHandler struct holding a store.Store (import the store package; hold the INTERFACE by value, never *store.Store), built by NewTaskHandler(s store.Store) *TaskHandler. http.HandlerFunc methods using the response helpers and r.PathValue("id"): Create (decodeJSON a models.Task, call its Validate() returning 400 on ErrInvalid via errors.Is, then store.CreateTask returning 201, or 409 on store.ErrExists); Get (200 or 404 on store.ErrNotFound); List (200 with the slice as JSON); Delete (204 or 404). Imports errors, net/http, and the store + models packages.
  - internal/api/projects.go: package api. ProjectHandler mirroring TaskHandler for projects: holds store.Store (by value), NewProjectHandler(s store.Store) *ProjectHandler, with Create (validate->400, CreateProject->201, 409 on store.ErrExists), Get (200/404), List (200), Delete (204/404). Reuse the response helpers. Imports errors, net/http, and the store + models packages.
  - internal/api/router.go: package api. func NewRouter(s store.Store, logger *slog.Logger) http.Handler that builds an http.ServeMux (NOT a third-party router), constructs the task and project handlers with s, and registers Go 1.22 METHOD+pattern routes (never a bare pattern twice): "POST /tasks", "GET /tasks", "GET /tasks/{id}", "DELETE /tasks/{id}", the same four for /projects, and "GET /healthz" -> 200 "ok". Wrap the mux with Chain(mux, Logging(logger), Recover(logger)) and return it. Imports log/slog, net/http, and the store package.
  - internal/api/router_test.go: package api. Integration tests (net/http/httptest) against NewRouter( store.NewMemStore(), slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(io.Discard, nil))). Import the store + models packages. Each case builds a FRESH router+store and a FRESH request body per request (never reuse a drained buffer). Cover: POST a valid task->201 and echo; invalid task (bad status)->400; duplicate id->409; GET missing->404; GET->200; list->200 JSON array; DELETE->204 then GET->404; malformed JSON (truncated, not empty {})->400; and a project create/get happy path. Imports bytes, encoding/json, io, log/slog, net/http, net/http/httptest, testing, and store + models.
  - cmd/server/main.go: package main — the composition root. Import config, store, api (their full module paths) and log/slog. func main() builds logger := slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(os.Stdout, nil)), cfg := config.Load(), s := store.NewMemStore(), handler := api.NewRouter(s, logger); constructs an *http.Server{Addr: cfg.Addr, Handler: handler, ReadTimeout/WriteTimeout from cfg}; runs it in a goroutine; and shuts down gracefully on os.Interrupt via signal.NotifyContext + srv.Shutdown with a context.WithTimeout(cfg.ShutdownTimeout). Log lifecycle events with slog. Imports context, errors, log/slog, net/http, os, os/signal, time, and the three internal packages.


OTHER-PACKAGE APIs — import these; only exported names are visible:
--- package main  (import "guildlm.dev/taskapi") ---

This project spans MULTIPLE PACKAGES (see the OTHER-PACKAGE APIs above). To use a symbol from another package, IMPORT that package by its full path (module path + '/' + its directory) and call it qualified as `pkgname.Symbol`. Only EXPORTED (capitalised) names are visible across packages. Do NOT redeclare another package's types locally. This file's own `package` clause must match its directory's package name.
CALL OTHER-PACKAGE FUNCTIONS WITH THEIR EXACT SIGNATURE shown above — do not add or drop a parameter (e.g. do not pass a context.Context to a method that does not take one, and DO pass it to one that does), and match the return arity. When you construct a struct, the field names in the literal MUST be the struct's declared field names.

IMPLEMENT EVERY function and method FULLY with real working logic — do NOT stub or leave placeholders (no `return ErrNotImplemented`, no `panic("not implemented")`, no TODO). If several methods are similar (e.g. the Project methods mirror the Task methods), write out each one completely. Never reference a symbol you have not defined or imported.

Write the complete contents of internal/config/config.go. Use only the Go standard library. Output one fenced ```go block.

Description: A real, production-shaped, STANDARD-LIBRARY-ONLY Go backend laid out as a proper multi-package project (cmd/ + internal/), the way an experienced Go engineer structures a service. No external dependencies whatsoever: net/http with its Go 1.22 ServeMux method routing, log/slog for STRUCTURED logging, context for cancellation, encoding/json, sync. Clean layering with dependency injection: config -> models -> store (repository interface + memory impl) -> api (handlers + middleware + router) -> cmd/server (composition root). Each package has ONE clear responsibility; packages depend inward only. Cross-package symbols are used by importing the package and qualifying them (models.Task, store.Store, store.ErrNotFound). Only exported (capitalised) names cross package boundaries.
Go module path: guildlm.dev/taskapi

TARGET_FILE: internal/store/store.go
Purpose of this file: package store. The repository abstraction. Import the models package (guildlm.dev/taskapi/internal/models) and use models.Task / models.Project (qualified — do NOT redeclare them here). Exported sentinels var ErrNotFound and var ErrExists (errors.New, matchable with errors.Is). A Store INTERFACE with CreateTask(models.Task) error, GetTask(id string) (models.Task, error), ListTasks() []models.Task, DeleteTask(id string) error, and the same four for Project. Imports errors and the models package only.

  - go.mod: Go module file. Module path guildlm.dev/taskapi, go 1.23. No dependencies. Output as a ```mod block, not Go.
  - internal/config/config.go: package config. A Config struct { Addr string; ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, ShutdownTimeout time.Duration } and func Load() Config that reads env vars (os.Getenv) with sensible defaults (Addr ":8080", timeouts 5s/10s/10s), parsing durations/addresses defensively. Self-contained: imports only os, time (and strconv if needed). No other project package.
  - internal/models/models.go: package models. Domain types and their validation. Task struct {ID, Title, Status, ProjectID string} and Project struct {ID, Name string}, all with JSON tags. Valid Task.Status is "todo"|"doing"|"done". A sentinel var ErrInvalid = errors.New("invalid") (exported, matchable with errors.Is). Methods (t Task) Validate() error and (p Project) Validate() error that wrap ErrInvalid via fmt.Errorf("%w: ...") when Title/Name is empty or Status is not allowed. Imports only errors, fmt. No other project package.
  - internal/store/store.go: package store. The repository abstraction. Import the models package (guildlm.dev/taskapi/internal/models) and use models.Task / models.Project (qualified — do NOT redeclare them here). Exported sentinels var ErrNotFound and var ErrExists (errors.New, matchable with errors.Is). A Store INTERFACE with CreateTask(models.Task) error, GetTask(id string) (models.Task, error), ListTasks() []models.Task, DeleteTask(id string) error, and the same four for Project. Imports errors and the models package only.
  - internal/store/memory.go: package store. A goroutine-safe in-memory implementation of the Store interface from store.go (same package — reference Store, ErrNotFound, ErrExists directly, do NOT redeclare them). MemStore struct holding two maps (tasks, projects) keyed by ID guarded by one sync.RWMutex, built by NewMemStore() *MemStore. Create returns ErrExists on duplicate id; Get/Delete return ErrNotFound; List returns items sorted by ID. Use models.Task/models.Project (import the models package). A compile-time var _ Store = (*MemStore)(nil) assertion. Imports sort, sync, and the models package.
  - internal/store/memory_test.go: package store. Table/flow tests for MemStore (same package — call NewMemStore, Store methods, ErrNotFound, ErrExists directly; use models.Task via the imported models package). Each case builds a FRESH NewMemStore (never shared). Cover create->nil then duplicate->ErrExists (errors.Is); Get present->value / missing->ErrNotFound; List is id-sorted; Delete present->nil then Get->ErrNotFound; Delete missing->ErrNotFound. Run race-clean. Imports errors, testing, and the models package.
  - internal/api/response.go: package api. Small HTTP/JSON helpers reused by every handler: writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v any) sets Content-Type application/json and encodes v; writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, msg string) writes a JSON {"error": msg}; decodeJSON(r *http.Request, dst any) error decodes the body and returns an error on malformed input. Imports encoding/json, net/http only. No other project package.
  - internal/api/middleware.go: package api. Middleware type func(http.Handler) http.Handler and Chain(h http.Handler, mws ...Middleware) http.Handler where the first listed is outermost. A Logging(logger *slog.Logger) Middleware that logs method, path, resolved status (via a statusRecorder wrapping http.ResponseWriter, default 200) and duration using STRUCTURED log/slog (logger.Info with slog attributes). A Recover(logger *slog.Logger) Middleware turning a downstream panic into a 500 and logging it. Imports log/slog, net/http, time. No other project package.
  - internal/api/tasks.go: package api. TaskHandler struct holding a store.Store (import the store package; hold the INTERFACE by value, never *store.Store), built by NewTaskHandler(s store.Store) *TaskHandler. http.HandlerFunc methods using the response helpers and r.PathValue("id"): Create (decodeJSON a models.Task, call its Validate() returning 400 on ErrInvalid via errors.Is, then store.CreateTask returning 201, or 409 on store.ErrExists); Get (200 or 404 on store.ErrNotFound); List (200 with the slice as JSON); Delete (204 or 404). Imports errors, net/http, and the store + models packages.
  - internal/api/projects.go: package api. ProjectHandler mirroring TaskHandler for projects: holds store.Store (by value), NewProjectHandler(s store.Store) *ProjectHandler, with Create (validate->400, CreateProject->201, 409 on store.ErrExists), Get (200/404), List (200), Delete (204/404). Reuse the response helpers. Imports errors, net/http, and the store + models packages.
  - internal/api/router.go: package api. func NewRouter(s store.Store, logger *slog.Logger) http.Handler that builds an http.ServeMux (NOT a third-party router), constructs the task and project handlers with s, and registers Go 1.22 METHOD+pattern routes (never a bare pattern twice): "POST /tasks", "GET /tasks", "GET /tasks/{id}", "DELETE /tasks/{id}", the same four for /projects, and "GET /healthz" -> 200 "ok". Wrap the mux with Chain(mux, Logging(logger), Recover(logger)) and return it. Imports log/slog, net/http, and the store package.
  - internal/api/router_test.go: package api. Integration tests (net/http/httptest) against NewRouter( store.NewMemStore(), slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(io.Discard, nil))). Import the store + models packages. Each case builds a FRESH router+store and a FRESH request body per request (never reuse a drained buffer). Cover: POST a valid task->201 and echo; invalid task (bad status)->400; duplicate id->409; GET missing->404; GET->200; list->200 JSON array; DELETE->204 then GET->404; malformed JSON (truncated, not empty {})->400; and a project create/get happy path. Imports bytes, encoding/json, io, log/slog, net/http, net/http/httptest, testing, and store + models.
  - cmd/server/main.go: package main — the composition root. Import config, store, api (their full module paths) and log/slog. func main() builds logger := slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(os.Stdout, nil)), cfg := config.Load(), s := store.NewMemStore(), handler := api.NewRouter(s, logger); constructs an *http.Server{Addr: cfg.Addr, Handler: handler, ReadTimeout/WriteTimeout from cfg}; runs it in a goroutine; and shuts down gracefully on os.Interrupt via signal.NotifyContext + srv.Shutdown with a context.WithTimeout(cfg.ShutdownTimeout). Log lifecycle events with slog. Imports context, errors, log/slog, net/http, os, os/signal, time, and the three internal packages.

package store
var ErrNotFound
var ErrExists
type Store interface {
	CreateTask(models.Task) error
	GetTask(id string) (models.Task, error)
	ListTasks() []models.Task
	DeleteTask(id string) error
	CreateProject(models.Project) error
	GetProject(id string) (models.Project, error)
	ListProjects() []models.Project
	DeleteProject(id string) error
}
type MemStore struct {
	mu       sync.RWMutex
	tasks    map[string]models.Task
	projects map[string]models.Project
}
func NewMemStore() *MemStore
func (s *MemStore) CreateTask(t models.Task) error
func (s *MemStore) GetTask(id string) (models.Task, error)
func (s *MemStore) ListTasks() []models.Task
func (s *MemStore) DeleteTask(id string) error
func (s *MemStore) CreateProject(p models.Project) error
func (s *MemStore) GetProject(id string) (models.Project, error)
func (s *MemStore) ListProjects() []models.Project
func (s *MemStore) DeleteProject(id string) error

No external dependencies whatsoever: net/http with its Go 1.22 ServeMux method routing, log/slog for STRUCTURED logging, context for cancellation, encoding/json, sync. Clean layering with dependency injection: config -> models -> store (repository interface + memory impl) -> api (handlers + middleware + router) -> cmd/server (composition root). Each package has ONE clear responsibility; packages depend inward only. Cross-package symbols are used by importing the package and qualifying them (models.Task, store.Store, store.ErrNotFound). Only exported (capitalised) names cross package boundaries.
Go module path: guildlm.dev/taskapi

TARGET_FILE: internal/api/tasks.go
Purpose of this file: package api. TaskHandler struct holding a store.Store (import the store package; hold the INTERFACE by value, never *store.Store), built by NewTaskHandler(s store.Store) *TaskHandler. http.HandlerFunc methods using the response helpers and r.PathValue("id"): Create (decodeJSON a models.Task, call its Validate() returning 400 on ErrInvalid via errors.Is, then store.CreateTask returning 201, or 409 on store.ErrExists); Get (200 or 404 on store.ErrNotFound); List (200 with the slice as JSON); Delete (204 or 404). Imports errors, net/http, and the store + models packages.

All files in this project:
  - go.mod: Go module file. Module path guildlm.dev/taskapi, go 1.23. No dependencies. Output as a ```mod block, not Go.
  - internal/config/config.go: package config. A Config struct { Addr string; ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, ShutdownTimeout time.Duration } and func Load() Config that reads env vars (os.Getenv) with sensible defaults (Addr ":8080", timeouts 5s/10s/10s), parsing durations/addresses defensively. Self-contained: imports only os, time (and strconv if needed). No other project package.
  - internal/models/models.go: package models. Domain types and their validation. Task struct {ID, Title, Status, ProjectID string} and Project struct {ID, Name string}, all with JSON tags. Valid Task.Status is "todo"|"doing"|"done". A sentinel var ErrInvalid = errors.New("invalid") (exported, matchable with errors.Is). Methods (t Task) Validate() error and (p Project) Validate() error that wrap ErrInvalid via fmt.Errorf("%w: ...") when Title/Name is empty or Status is not allowed. Imports only errors, fmt. No other project package.
  - internal/store/store.go: package store. The repository abstraction. Import the models package (guildlm.dev/taskapi/internal/models) and use models.Task / models.Project (qualified — do NOT redeclare them here). Exported sentinels var ErrNotFound and var ErrExists (errors.New, matchable with errors.Is). A Store INTERFACE with CreateTask(models.Task) error, GetTask(id string) (models.Task, error), ListTasks() []models.Task, DeleteTask(id string) error, and the same four for Project. Imports errors and the models package only.
  - internal/store/memory.go: package store. A goroutine-safe in-memory implementation of the Store interface from store.go (same package — reference Store, ErrNotFound, ErrExists directly, do NOT redeclare them). MemStore struct holding two maps (tasks, projects) keyed by ID guarded by one sync.RWMutex, built by NewMemStore() *MemStore. Create returns ErrExists on duplicate id; Get/Delete return ErrNotFound; List returns items sorted by ID. Use models.Task/models.Project (import the models package). A compile-time var _ Store = (*MemStore)(nil) assertion. Imports sort, sync, and the models package.
  - internal/store/memory_test.go: package store. Table/flow tests for MemStore (same package — call NewMemStore, Store methods, ErrNotFound, ErrExists directly; use models.Task via the imported models package). Each case builds a FRESH NewMemStore (never shared). Cover create->nil then duplicate->ErrExists (errors.Is); Get present->value / missing->ErrNotFound; List is id-sorted; Delete present->nil then Get->ErrNotFound; Delete missing->ErrNotFound. Run race-clean. Imports errors, testing, and the models package.
  - internal/api/response.go: package api. Small HTTP/JSON helpers reused by every handler: writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v any) sets Content-Type application/json and encodes v; writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, msg string) writes a JSON {"error": msg}; decodeJSON(r *http.Request, dst any) error decodes the body and returns an error on malformed input. Imports encoding/json, net/http only. No other project package.
  - internal/api/middleware.go: package api. Middleware type func(http.Handler) http.Handler and Chain(h http.Handler, mws ...Middleware) http.Handler where the first listed is outermost. A Logging(logger *slog.Logger) Middleware that logs method, path, resolved status (via a statusRecorder wrapping http.ResponseWriter, default 200) and duration using STRUCTURED log/slog (logger.Info with slog attributes). A Recover(logger *slog.Logger) Middleware turning a downstream panic into a 500 and logging it. Imports log/slog, net/http, time. No other project package.
  - internal/api/tasks.go: package api. TaskHandler struct holding a store.Store (import the store package; hold the INTERFACE by value, never *store.Store), built by NewTaskHandler(s store.Store) *TaskHandler. http.HandlerFunc methods using the response helpers and r.PathValue("id"): Create (decodeJSON a models.Task, call its Validate() returning 400 on ErrInvalid via errors.Is, then store.CreateTask returning 201, or 409 on store.ErrExists); Get (200 or 404 on store.ErrNotFound); List (200 with the slice as JSON); Delete (204 or 404). Imports errors, net/http, and the store + models packages.
  - internal/api/projects.go: package api. ProjectHandler mirroring TaskHandler for projects: holds store.Store (by value), NewProjectHandler(s store.Store) *ProjectHandler, with Create (validate->400, CreateProject->201, 409 on store.ErrExists), Get (200/404), List (200), Delete (204/404). Reuse the response helpers. Imports errors, net/http, and the store + models packages.
  - internal/api/router.go: package api. func NewRouter(s store.Store, logger *slog.Logger) http.Handler that builds an http.ServeMux (NOT a third-party router), constructs the task and project handlers with s, and registers Go 1.22 METHOD+pattern routes (never a bare pattern twice): "POST /tasks", "GET /tasks", "GET /tasks/{id}", "DELETE /tasks/{id}", the same four for /projects, and "GET /healthz" -> 200 "ok". Wrap the mux with Chain(mux, Logging(logger), Recover(logger)) and return it. Imports log/slog, net/http, and the store package.
  - internal/api/router_test.go: package api. Integration tests (net/http/httptest) against NewRouter( store.NewMemStore(), slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(io.Discard, nil))). Import the store + models packages. Each case builds a FRESH router+store and a FRESH request body per request (never reuse a drained buffer). Cover: POST a valid task->201 and echo; invalid task (bad status)->400; duplicate id->409; GET missing->404; GET->200; list->200 JSON array; DELETE->204 then GET->404; malformed JSON (truncated, not empty {})->400; and a project create/get happy path. Imports bytes, encoding/json, io, log/slog, net/http, net/http/httptest, testing, and store + models.
  - cmd/server/main.go: package main — the composition root. Import config, store, api (their full module paths) and log/slog. func main() builds logger := slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(os.Stdout, nil)), cfg := config.Load(), s := store.NewMemStore(), handler := api.NewRouter(s, logger); constructs an *http.Server{Addr: cfg.Addr, Handler: handler, ReadTimeout/WriteTimeout from cfg}; runs it in a goroutine; and shuts down gracefully on os.Interrupt via signal.NotifyContext + srv.Shutdown with a context.WithTimeout(cfg.ShutdownTimeout). Log lifecycle events with slog. Imports context, errors, log/slog, net/http, os, os/signal, time, and the three internal packages.

```go
package store

import (
	"errors"

	"guildlm.dev/taskapi/internal/models"
)

var (
	ErrNotFound = errors.New("not found")
	ErrExists   = errors.New("already exists")
)

type Store interface {
	CreateTask(models.Task) error
	GetTask(id string) (models.Task, error)
	ListTasks() []models.Task
	DeleteTask(id string) error

	CreateProject(models.Project) error
	GetProject(id string) (models.Project, error)
	ListProjects() []models.Project
	DeleteProject(id string) error
}
```

	DeleteProject(id string) error
}

Project: taskapi
Language: go
Description: A real, production-shaped, STANDARD-LIBRARY-ONLY Go backend laid out as a proper multi-package project (cmd/ + internal/), the way an experienced Go engineer structures a service. No external dependencies whatsoever: net/http with its Go 1.22 ServeMux method routing, log/slog for STRUCTURED logging, context for cancellation, encoding/json, sync. Clean layering with dependency injection: config -> models -> store (repository interface + memory impl) -> api (handlers + middleware + router) -> cmd/server (composition root). Each package has ONE clear responsibility; packages depend inward only. Cross-package symbols are used by importing the package and qualifying them (models.Task, store.Store, store.ErrNotFound). Only exported (capitalised) names cross package boundaries.
Go module path: guildlm.dev/taskapi

TARGET_FILE: internal/api/tasks.go
Purpose of this file: package api. TaskHandler struct holding a store.Store (import the store package; hold the INTERFACE by value, never *store.Store), built by NewTaskHandler(s store.Store) *TaskHandler. http.HandlerFunc methods using the response helpers and r.PathValue("id"): Create (decodeJSON a models.Task, call its Validate() returning 400 on ErrInvalid via errors.Is, then store.CreateTask returning 201, or 409 on store.ErrExists); Get (200 or 404 on store.ErrNotFound); List (200 with the slice as JSON); Delete (204 or 404). Imports errors, net/http, and the store + models packages.

All files in this project:
  - go.mod: Go module file. Module path guildlm.dev/taskapi, go 1.23. No dependencies. Output as a ```mod block, not Go.
  - internal/config/config.go: package config. A Config struct { Addr string; ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, ShutdownTimeout time.Duration } and func Load() Config that reads env vars (os.Getenv) with sensible defaults (Addr ":8080", timeouts 5s/10s/10s), parsing durations/addresses defensively. Self-contained: imports only os, time (and strconv if needed). No other project package.
  - internal/models/models.go: package models. Domain types and their validation. Task struct {ID, Title, Status, ProjectID string} and Project struct {ID, Name string}, all with JSON tags. Valid Task.Status is "todo"|"doing"|"done". A sentinel var ErrInvalid = errors.New("invalid") (exported, matchable with errors.Is). Methods (t Task) Validate() error and (p Project) Validate() error that wrap ErrInvalid via fmt.Errorf("%w: ...") when Title/Name is empty or Status is not allowed. Imports only errors, fmt. No other project package.
  - internal/store/store.go: package store. The repository abstraction. Import the models package (guildlm.dev/taskapi/internal/models) and use models.Task / models.Project (qualified — do NOT redeclare them here). Exported sentinels var ErrNotFound and var ErrExists (errors.New, matchable with errors.Is). A Store INTERFACE with CreateTask(models.Task) error, GetTask(id string) (models.Task, error), ListTasks() []models.Task, DeleteTask(id string) error, and the same four for Project. Imports errors and the models package only.
  - internal/store/memory.go: package store. A goroutine-safe in-memory implementation of the Store interface from store.go (same package — reference Store, ErrNotFound, ErrExists directly, do NOT redeclare them). MemStore struct holding two maps (tasks, projects) keyed by ID guarded by one sync.RWMutex, built by NewMemStore() *MemStore. Create returns ErrExists on duplicate id; Get/Delete return ErrNotFound; List returns items sorted by ID. Use models.Task/models.Project (import the models package). A compile-time var _ Store = (*MemStore)(nil) assertion. Imports sort, sync, and the models package.
  - internal/store/memory_test.go: package store. Table/flow tests for MemStore (same package — call NewMemStore, Store methods, ErrNotFound, ErrExists directly; use models.Task via the imported models package). Each case builds a FRESH NewMemStore (never shared). Cover create->nil then duplicate->ErrExists (errors.Is); Get present->value / missing->ErrNotFound; List is id-sorted; Delete present->nil then Get->ErrNotFound; Delete missing->ErrNotFound. Run race-clean. Imports errors, testing, and the models package.
  - internal/api/response.go: package api. Small HTTP/JSON helpers reused by every handler: writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v any) sets Content-Type application/json and encodes v; writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, msg string) writes a JSON {"error": msg}; decodeJSON(r *http.Request, dst any) error decodes the body and returns an error on malformed input. Imports encoding/json, net/http only. No other project package.
  - internal/api/middleware.go: package api. Middleware type func(http.Handler) http.Handler and Chain(h http.Handler, mws ...Middleware) http.Handler where the first listed is outermost. A Logging(logger *slog.Logger) Middleware that logs method, path, resolved status (via a statusRecorder wrapping http.ResponseWriter, default 200) and duration using STRUCTURED log/slog (logger.Info with slog attributes). A Recover(logger *slog.Logger) Middleware turning a downstream panic into a 500 and logging it. Imports log/slog, net/http, time. No other project package.
  - internal/api/tasks.go: package api. TaskHandler struct holding a store.Store (import the store package; hold the INTERFACE by value, never *store.Store), built by NewTaskHandler(s store.Store) *TaskHandler. http.HandlerFunc methods using the response helpers and r.PathValue("id"): Create (decodeJSON a models.Task, call its Validate() returning 400 on ErrInvalid via errors.Is, then store.CreateTask returning 201, or 409 on store.ErrExists); Get (200 or 404 on store.ErrNotFound); List (200 with the slice as JSON); Delete (204 or 404). Imports errors, net/http, and the store + models packages.
  - internal/api/projects.go: package api. ProjectHandler mirroring TaskHandler for projects: holds store.Store (by value), NewProjectHandler(s store.Store) *ProjectHandler, with Create (validate->400, CreateProject->201, 409 on store.ErrExists), Get (200/404), List (200), Delete (204/404). Reuse the response helpers. Imports errors, net/http, and the store + models packages.
  - internal/api/router.go: package api. func NewRouter(s store.Store, logger *slog.Logger) http.Handler that builds an http.ServeMux (NOT a third-party router), constructs the task and project handlers with s, and registers Go 1.22 METHOD+pattern routes (never a bare pattern twice): "POST /tasks", "GET /tasks", "GET /tasks/{id}", "DELETE /tasks/{id}", the same four for /projects, and "GET /healthz" -> 200 "ok". Wrap the mux with Chain(mux, Logging(logger), Recover(logger)) and return it. Imports log/slog, net/http, and the store package.
  - internal/api/router_test.go: package api. Integration tests (net/http/httptest) against NewRouter( store.NewMemStore(), slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(io.Discard, nil))). Import the store + models packages. Each case builds a FRESH router+store and a FRESH request body per request (never reuse a drained buffer). Cover: POST a valid task->201 and echo; invalid task (bad status)->400; duplicate id->409; GET missing->404; GET->200; list->200 JSON array; DELETE->204 then GET->404; malformed JSON (truncated, not empty {})->400; and a project create/get happy path. Imports bytes, encoding/json, io, log/slog, net/http, net/http/httptest, testing, and store + models.
  - cmd/server/main.go: package main — the composition root. Import config, store, api (their full module paths) and log/slog. func main() builds logger := slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(os.Stdout, nil)), cfg := config.Load(), s := store.NewMemStore(), handler := api.NewRouter(s, logger); constructs an *http.Server{Addr: cfg.Addr, Handler: handler, ReadTimeout/WriteTimeout from cfg}; runs it in a goroutine; and shuts down gracefully on os.Interrupt via signal.NotifyContext + srv.Shutdown with a context.WithTimeout(cfg.ShutdownTimeout). Log lifecycle events with slog. Imports context, errors, log/slog, net/http, os, os/signal, time, and the three internal packages.

Project: taskflow
Language: go
Description: A larger, realistic, STANDARD-LIBRARY-ONLY HTTP backend for a task tracker — the kind of multi-resource, layered Go service that stresses cross-file consistency. No external dependencies whatsoever: net/http + ServeMux for routing (never a third-party router), encoding/json for bodies, sync for concurrency. All files use package main. Layers: sentinel errors, domain models with validation, a generic-ish in-memory repository, HTTP middleware, JSON + pagination helpers, two resource handlers (tasks and projects), a router that wires everything, and a main with graceful shutdown.
Go module path: guildlm.dev/taskflow

TARGET_FILE: errors.go
Purpose of this file: package main. Shared sentinel errors used across the store and handlers, all matchable with errors.Is: ErrNotFound, ErrExists, ErrValidation. Standard library only (errors).

  - go.mod: Go module file. Module path guildlm.dev/taskflow, go 1.23. No dependencies. Output as a ```mod block, not Go.

Valid Task.Status values are "todo", "doing", "done". A method (Task) Validate() error that returns a wrapped ErrValidation (use fmt.Errorf with %w) when Title is empty or Status is not one of the three allowed values; a (Project) Validate() error that returns ErrValidation when Name is empty. Standard library only.
  - store.go: package main. In-memory repository for BOTH resources. A Store struct holding two maps (tasks map[string]Task, projects map[string]Project) guarded by a single sync.RWMutex, built by NewStore(). Methods: CreateTask(Task) error / GetTask(id) (Task, error) / ListTasks() []Task / DeleteTask(id) error and the same four for Project. Create returns ErrExists on duplicate ID; Get/Delete return ErrNotFound. List methods return items sorted by ID. Standard library only (sync, sort, errors as needed).
  - middleware.go: package main. A Middleware type func(http.Handler) http.Handler and Chain(h, mws...) where the first listed is outermost. A Logging middleware (method, path, resolved status via a statusRecorder wrapping http.ResponseWriter defaulting to 200, and latency) and a Recover middleware (downstream panic -> 500). Standard library only.
  - httpjson.go: package main. HTTP/JSON helpers reused by every handler: writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v any) that sets Content-Type application/json and encodes v; writeError(w, status int, msg string) that writes a JSON {"error": msg}; and decodeJSON(r *http.Request, dst any) error that decodes the request body and returns an error on malformed input. Standard library only (encoding/json, net/http).
  - pagination.go: package main. func parsePage(r *http.Request) (limit, offset int) that reads ?limit= and ?offset= query params with strconv, defaulting limit to 20 (max 100) and offset to 0, clamping negatives to the default/zero. A GENERIC func paginate[T any](items []T, limit, offset int) []T that safely slices with bounds checks (offset past the end returns empty, never panics) — used for BOTH tasks and projects. Standard library only (net/http, strconv).
  - tasks_handler.go: package main. A TaskHandler struct holding *Store, built by NewTaskHandler(*Store). http.HandlerFunc methods using the httpjson + pagination helpers and r.PathValue("id"): Create (decodeJSON a Task, call Task.Validate returning 400 with writeError on ErrValidation via errors.Is, store it returning 201, 409 on ErrExists); List (parsePage then paginate(tasks, limit, offset), 200 with the page as JSON, optional ?status= filter); Get (200 or 404); Delete (204 or 404). Standard library only.
  - projects_handler.go: package main. A ProjectHandler struct holding *Store, built by NewProjectHandler(*Store). http.HandlerFunc methods mirroring the task handler for projects: Create (validate -> 400, store -> 201, 409), List (200 JSON array), Get (200/404), Delete (204/404). Reuse the httpjson helpers. Standard library only.
  - router.go: package main. func NewRouter(s *Store) http.Handler building an http.ServeMux (NOT a third-party router) wiring both handlers: POST/GET /tasks, GET/DELETE /tasks/{id}, POST/GET /projects, GET/DELETE /projects/{id}, and GET /health -> 200 "ok". Wrap with Chain(mux, Logging, Recover) and return it so tests can drive the fully-wired handler. Standard library only.
  - main.go: package main. func main() builds NewStore + NewRouter, runs an *http.Server on :8080 with graceful shutdown via signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt) and srv.Shutdown under a short context.WithTimeout. Standard library only.
  - taskflow_test.go: package main. Isolated table/flow tests (net/http/httptest) against NewRouter(NewStore()). Each case builds a FRESH router+store (never share state) and a FRESH request body per request (never reuse a drained buffer). Cover: create a task -> 201 and echo; invalid task (bad status) -> 400; duplicate task ID -> 409; GET missing -> 404; list tasks -> 200 with a JSON array; pagination (?limit=1) returns at most 1; delete -> 204 then GET -> 404; malformed JSON (truncated bytes, not empty {}) -> 400; and the same create/get happy-path for a project. Standard library only.

--- router.go ---
package main

import (
	"net/http"
)

// NewRouter builds an http.ServeMux mapping method+pattern routes to a UserHandler.
func NewRouter(s Store) http.Handler {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	userHandler := NewUserHandler(s)

	mux.HandleFunc("POST /users", userHandler.Create)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /users", userHandler.List)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /users/{id}", userHandler.Get)
	mux.HandleFunc("DELETE /users/{id}", userHandler.Delete)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /health", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		_, _ = w.Write([]byte("ok"))
	})

	return Chain(mux, Logging, Recover)
}

Project: pool
Language: go
Description: A tiny, idiomatic, STANDARD-LIBRARY-ONLY Go package "pool" providing a bounded-concurrency parallel map. No external dependencies. All files use package pool. Must be race-free.
Go module path: guildlm.dev/pool

TARGET_FILE: pool.go
Purpose of this file: package pool. ParallelMap[T any, R any](items []T, workers int, fn func(T) R) []R applies fn to every item using at most `workers` goroutines and returns results in INPUT ORDER. Treat workers <= 0 as 1. Must be race-free (each worker writes only its own result index; no shared mutable state needs a mutex). Use a jobs channel of indices, a sync.WaitGroup, and close the channel before Wait. Standard library only.
